title: Staunton in the Vale W.4270 Memorial
crew_summary: 7 killed including pilot WO2. Thomas Herbert Warne

sources.title: Aircrew Remembered - Sgt Warne, Lancaster W4270

narrative: Lancaster W4270 had been airborne for nearly seven hours on a long cross-country training exercise when disaster struck. The aircraft was brand new to 61 Squadron at RAF Syerston. At 22:56 hours, a connecting rod broke on the inner starboard engine, sparking a fire the crew could not extinguish. With the landing gear in a lowered position — possibly deployed in preparation for an emergency landing — the aircraft became uncontrollable and crashed into farmland northwest of Staunton-in-the-Vale church. All seven crew were killed instantly. The pilot, Sgt Thomas "Herb" Warne from Saskatchewan, was the oldest and most experienced at just 23, despite having only 15 hours of night flying on Lancasters. The youngest, flight engineer George Hitchon from Padiham, was 19. Three Commonwealth airmen were buried together at Newark Cemetery; the other four were returned to their home towns across Britain. The crash site lies in the agricultural lowlands south of Newark, within sight of the Lincolnshire border — territory that felt safe and familiar to crews who flew from Syerston daily, making the training loss especially poignant.

crew.name: Thomas Herbert Warne
crew.fate_detail: Killed instantly when the Lancaster crashed into farmland after an engine fire made the aircraft uncontrollable. Had only 15 hours of night flying on Lancasters.
crew.bio: Canadian pilot from Saskatchewan serving with the RCAF, aged 23. The most experienced crew member despite having only 15 hours night flying on Lancasters.

crew.name: George Arthur Hitchon
crew.fate_detail: Killed instantly in the crash. The youngest crew member at 19.
crew.bio: Flight engineer from Padiham, Lancashire, aged 19. The youngest of the crew.

crew.name: Robert John Preece
crew.burial_location: Wembdon Churchyard, Sec. C, Row A, Grave 14
crew.bio: Observer serving with the RAF, aged 22.

crew.name: Thomas Raine Newton
crew.bio: Wireless operator/air gunner serving with the RAF, aged 21.

crew.name: James Milton Whitehead
crew.bio: Wireless operator/air gunner serving with the RAF, aged 22.

crew.name: Edward John Loverock
crew.bio: Air gunner serving with the RAF, aged 21.

crew.name: John Coaker
crew.bio: Air gunner serving with the RAF, aged 22. Buried in Leusdon Churchyard, Devon.

cause_of_loss: Con rod broke on inner starboard engine; fire spread; undercarriage lowered causing loss of control
memorial_details: An original memorial was erected in St Mary's churchyard, Staunton-in-the-Vale. By 2010, the original brass plaques had degraded, and local historian efforts led to their replacement with slate tablets. A rededication ceremony was held on 3 July 2010, attended by relatives and RAF representatives. The memorial site is publicly accessible in the churchyard. A dedicated website (stauntonlancaster.co.uk) preserves the full story of the crew.

maintainer.organisation: Staunton in the Vale Parish Meeting
maintainer.notes: Memorial with interpretation board in new burial ground of St Mary's Church.

external_narratives.summary: Detailed biographical page for the crew of Lancaster W4270 from 61 Squadron. Includes individual crew histories, service details, and burial information for all seven airmen.
external_narratives.key_details: Confirms pilot was WO2 Thomas Herbert Warne, RCAF, from Saskatchewan. Aircraft was on a long cross-country training exercise from RAF Syerston when a con rod failure caused the crash.

external_narratives.summary: Local history account of the crash and memorial at Staunton-in-the-Vale. Details the 2010 rededication ceremony when new plaques replaced the degraded originals, with relatives of the crew attending.
external_narratives.key_details: Memorial originally built for the millennium exhibition. Contact was made with the last surviving relatives during the 2010 rededication.

external_narratives.summary: International crash site database entry documenting the Lancaster W4270 memorial at Staunton in the Vale, with location details and basic incident information.
external_narratives.key_details: Lists the memorial as a crash site marker type with location coordinates and connection to 61 Squadron RAF Syerston.

external_narratives.summary: Imperial War Museums register entry for the Lancaster W4270 crew memorial at Staunton in the Vale, catalogued as memorial reference 61723.
external_narratives.key_details: Formally registered memorial in the IWM national war memorials database.

external_narratives.summary: Commonwealth War Graves Commission casualty record for Warrant Officer Class II Thomas Herbert Warne, confirming his service with RCAF and burial at Newark-upon-Trent Cemetery.
external_narratives.key_details: CWGC casualty record number 2703186. Confirms burial location and RCAF service number R/102085.

local_contacts.org_name: Staunton in the Vale Parish Meeting
local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep
